input.initialPrompt: I have an existing xUnit test project at ./test-output/MyTests/MyTests.csproj. Create a reusable template from it so my team can use it for new test projects. The template should be called 'Team Test Project'.

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to create a .NET template from an existing xUnit test project. Let me first explore the existing project to understand its structure, then create the necessary template configuration.

1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net9.0</TargetFramework>
4.     <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
5.     <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
6.     <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
7.     <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
8.   </PropertyGroup>
9.   <ItemGroup>
10.     <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.0" />
11.     <PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.9.0" />
12.     <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2">
13.       <IncludeAssets>runtime; build; native; contentfiles; analyzers; buildtransitive</IncludeAssets>
14.       <PrivateAssets>all</PrivateAssets>
15.     </PackageReference>
16.   </ItemGroup>
17. </Project>
18.

data.content: Now I'll create the `.template.config/template.json` to turn this into a reusable `dotnet new` template, with the project/namespace name parameterized.
data.reasoningText: Now I need to create a `.template.config/template.json` file inside the project directory. This is how .NET templates work. I also need to make the namespace and project name parameterizable using source name replacement.

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-8c568fc6f7184d80ad2fc44311fc221c/test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json b/tmp/sv-8c568fc6f7184d80ad2fc44311fc221c/test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-8c568fc6f7184d80ad2fc44311fc221c/test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json
@@ -1,0 +1,15 @@
+{
+  "$schema": "http://json.schemastore.org/template",
+  "author": "Team",
+  "classifications": ["Test", "xUnit"],
+  "identity": "TeamTestProject",
+  "name": "Team Test Project",
+  "shortName": "team-test",
+  "tags": {
+    "language": "C#",
+    "type": "project"
+  },
+  "sourceName": "MyTests",
+  "preferNameDirectory": true
+}
+

===csproj===
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net9.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.9.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2">
      <IncludeAssets>runtime; build; native; contentfiles; analyzers; buildtransitive</IncludeAssets>
      <PrivateAssets>all</PrivateAssets>
    </PackageReference>
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
===cs===
using Xunit;

namespace SampleTests;
public class UnitTest1
{

    public void Test1() { }
}

data.content:
The template is working correctly. The `sourceName` replacement substituted `MyTests` → `SampleTests` in both the `.csproj` filename and the `namespace` in the source file.

**What was created:**
- `test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json`

**How your team uses it:**

```bash
# Install the template (once)
dotnet new install ./test-output/MyTests

# Create a new test project
dotnet new team-test -n MyNewTests -o ./path/to/MyNewTests
```

The `-n` flag controls the project name and namespace — all occurrences of `MyTests` are automatically replaced.
